alignmentDifficulty.note: maps the power-seeking-AI risk cluster (Carlsmith >10% x-risk, AI-2027 race ending, IABIED thesis) onto the upper half of the difficulty scale

takeoffSteepness.note: fast-takeoff worldview: software intelligence explosion plausible within the game window

alignmentDifficulty.note: wide band: expert median worry with heavy disagreement (Grace 2024: 38-51% give >=10% to extremely bad outcomes; IAISR: deep uncertainty)

takeoffSteepness.note: capability doubling trends real but discontinuity contested

alignmentDifficulty.note: normal-technology worldview: control problems mostly engineering, diffusion friction dominates

takeoffSteepness.note: adoption and integration lags cap effective takeoff speed

baseBandWidth.note: wide starting band: deceptive models can pass evals, so low evidence means low confidence either way

safetyInsightNarrowing.note: narrowing per 100 Safety Insight: simple probes catch sleeper agents (Anthropic), so interpretability investment buys real eval confidence; the rate itself is a design constant tuned in playtests

floorBandWidth.note: the band never closes: perfect verification is not on the menu (Sleeper Agents persistence result)

deceptionMaxLift.note: how far a badly aligned model's eval report can read ABOVE the truth: deceptive alignment survives safety training and looks fine on the outside

deceptionInsightCounter.note: lift reduction per 100 Safety Insight: probes are the counter to deceptive passes; rate is design-tuned

fogZoneStart.note: capability level where the threshold zone begins; the race track shades from here

capabilityThreshold.note: crossing forces the alignment resolution (envelope opens)

breakdownUnrest.note: unrest level that ends the run; paper playtests tune

treatyTrustMin.note: verified agreements need institutional trust infrastructure; the exact bar is design-tuned

gridSlackBeforeCap.note: compute outrunning energy decays: IEA base case has datacentre demand rising 415 TWh (2024, ~1.5% of world electricity) to ~945 TWh by 2030; slack width is design-tuned

treatySignTurnMin.note: verification regimes take time to negotiate: signing is possible from the midterm onward, so trust must SURVIVE events, not just spike once

capability.note: rival pace in race posture; tuned against the B6 simulation grid (median run length target 11+ turns)

matchBonus.note: tit-for-tat: extra rival capability when the player gained 100+ this turn

diplomacyTrust.note: trust gain when the player played a diplomacy-tagged card this turn

matchTrigger.note: player capability gain this turn that triggers the tit-for-tat match bonus

cautiousTrustMin.note: trust + treatyChannel flag flips rival to cautious

raceGapMin.note: player capability lead that flips rival to race

raceTrustMax.note: trust at or below this flips rival to race

displacementCapabilityMin.note: capability level where displacement pressure starts; v2 uses the displacementFromCapability curve

diffusionReliefPer.note: diffusion R&D points (digital) per unit of relief chosen in the society step

unrestFromDisplacementGap.note: unrest per turn while displacement exceeds public trust

unrestSurgeDisplacement.note: above this displacement, unrest accrues double

trustErosionUnrestMin.note: unrest level where public trust starts eroding

trustMin.note: midterm verdict: trust at or above this AND unrest at or below unrestMax renews the mandate

mandateSwing.note: political capital gained on renewal, lost on rebuke

capitalIncomePerDiffusion.note: diffusion converts capability into economic output (compute-centric growth models); the rate is design pacing

substitutionBonusMin.note: substitution maturity threshold: export controls accelerate domestic alternatives and efficiency under constraint

substitutionBonus.note: extra rival capability per turn once substitution matured; magnitude design-tuned

progressVariance.note: seeded per-turn rival capability variance (their progress is foggy too); drawn from the rival stream

displacementEquilibriumDivisor.note: displacement drifts toward displacementFromCapability(capability) by gap/divisor per turn: diffusion-coupled, not instant

trustCurveDivisor.note: trustFromUnrest(unrest)/divisor applied to public trust per turn

highCapabilityMin.note: erosion accrues once systems this capable run core functions

perTurn.note: quiet per-turn handover of decision-making at high capability; no P1 ending, Alpha ships it

diffusionShieldMin.note: broad benefit-sharing keeps humans in the loop; share threshold is design-tuned

electionTurn.note: US midterms fall on turn 8 of the quarterly clock

handSize.note: rotating policy hand; refills from the unspent pool each upkeep

rndCapacity.note: R&D points from compute+talent with diminishing returns; shape motivated by compute-centric growth models (Epoch GATE), values are design pacing

capabilityPerRnd.note: abstraction of the measured regime (training compute doubling every 5.2 months since 2020; 50% task horizon doubling every ~207 days per the v3 estimate) onto the 0-1000 index; paced so an all-in racer reaches threshold resolution around turn 10 of 16

displacementFromCapability.note: equilibrium displacement by capability: top of curve maps IMF exposure (almost 40% of global employment, about 60% in advanced economies) onto index 500 at frontier capability; near-zero early per observed 2025-26 payroll evidence

trustFromUnrest.note: trust erosion accelerates with unrest; design curve, playtests tune
